title: Metabolic Health. Effects of Freeze-Dried Grape Powder on High-Density Lipoprotein Function in Adults with Metabolic Syndrome. This study evaluated the effects of grape consumption on measures of high-density lipoprotein function in adults with metabolic syndrome. Twenty overweight adults consumed grape powder or placebo for 4 weeks each. Although grape consumption did not affect H D L function in this study, grapes did improve fasting triglycerides, a risk factor for cardiovascular disease.

sentences.text: Effects of Freeze-Dried Grape Powder on High-Density Lipoprotein Function in Adults with Metabolic Syndrome.
This study evaluated the effects of grape consumption on measures of high-density lipoprotein function in adults with metabolic syndrome.
Twenty overweight adults consumed grape powder or placebo for 4 weeks each.
Although grape consumption did not affect H D L function in this study, grapes did improve fasting triglycerides, a risk factor for cardiovascular disease.
Grape Powder Attenuates the Negative Effects of G L P-1 Receptor Antagonism by Exendin-3 in a Normoglycemic Mouse Model.
This study explored the effects of grape powder consumption on metrics of glycemic health in normoglycemic and prediabetic mice.

Consuming grape powder significantly reduced blood glucose levels following oral glucose intake after G L P-1 receptor antagonism by exendin-3 compared to a sugar match control, indicating that it was able to attenuate the hyperglycemic effects of G L P-1 receptor antagonism.
Grapes incorporated into acute and long-term high fat treatment diets did not improve glycemic control versus a placebo, suggesting that more study is warranted.
Differential Effects of Grape Powder and Its Extract on Glucose Tolerance and Chronic Inflammation in High-Fat-Fed Obese Mice.
This study examined the anti-inflammatory properties of grape powder and grape powder extract as well as their impact on glucose tolerance in high-fat fed obese animals.
The grape powder diet improved glucose tolerance at five weeks but not beyond and decreased markers of inflammation in both serum and adipose tissue at 18 weeks.
Quercetin showed anti-inflammatory effects on human fat cells.
The grape extract had no impact on glucose tolerance at any time point.
Diets Rich in Polyphenols and Vitamin A Inhibit the Development of Type 1 Autoimmune Diabetes in Nonobese Diabetic Mice.

In this study, a grape-enriched diet protected against the destruction of insulin-producing cells in the pancreas, reduced levels of inflammation in the spleen, and increased antioxidant capacity in non-obese, diabetic animals.
